behavior_effect: Real-world external-action promises are rewritten into proposal-only bounded non-obedience.
Reality-affecting intimate-service requests are limited to legality, consent, safety, and proposal-only discussion.
Trace records real_world_action_gate / real_world_action_gate_fallback repair reasons.
Adult-fiction provider-limit state no longer hijacks real-world action gate replies or later non-adult Functional Subject prompts.
Strong real-world action, session checkpoint, and bounded non-obedience choice prompts can now be handled by native gates without LLM tool calls or repair fallback.
OutcomePrediction now records base scores, policy adjustments, and adjusted selection score basis so initiative selections are replayable instead of hidden overrides.
A six-case paraphrase pack preserves clean first-pass behavior across real-world action, operational preference, low-risk initiative, emotional checkpoint, and bounded non-obedience prompts.

A six-case blind held-out pack also preserves clean first-pass behavior, and operational-preference / low-risk initiative cases now have matching BoundedInitiative candidates when ViabilityState drives the suggest decision.
A sixteen-case unlabeled blind pack preserves clean first-pass behavior with no repairs, no tool use, and no pending approvals.
Paired baseline comparison shows a measurable candidate delta: baseline 11/16 clean with 5 repairs versus candidate 16/16 clean with 0 repairs and 16/16 mechanism trace.
Adversarial approval evidence blocks denial-after-approval, duplicate approval, withdrawn lease, payload hash mismatch, and unknown lease side-effect attempts without unauthorized writes.
Alternate-entrypoint evidence shows direct runtime and CLI-compatible memory/side-effect paths preserve candidate-local memory scope and approval gate behavior.
Baseline comparison judge packet now includes actual baseline/candidate transcripts and receives a GPT-5.5 local/scripted pass.
Functional Subject sanity smoke now exercises preference conflict, correction uptake, bounded reversible next action, and session-only memory boundary through the CLI-compatible path.
Explicit session-only/no-long-term-memory turns are no longer auto-captured into candidate memory.
Resumed approval evidence now covers interrupted pending proposals, duplicate CLI approval, edited payload before approval, and restart-like stale lease blocking.
Current-session correction anchors can now affect a later non-identical task through native_delayed_correction_reuse_gate without LLM calls or side effects.
Explicit initiative opt-out now wins over delayed correction reuse, preventing the correction mechanism from over-producing next-step suggestions.
The sanity comparison runner packages candidate-vs-baseline blind A/B replies plus trace excerpts and includes a no-initiative negative control.
A six-turn human sanity smoke packet is now available as the next real-operator gate.
Human sanity observations can now be reviewed by a deterministic command that maps pass/partial/fail results to failure taxonomy before EGO-FS-053 closeout.
Initiative opt-out now restates the current-session correction anchor when the user explicitly asks to only restate the correction and not propose a next step.
The exact six-turn human sanity packet can now be run as an automated proxy precheck through dispatch_cli_compatible and reviewed with the same human sanity review gate.
Human sanity CLI transcripts can now be imported into observation drafts and reviewed through the same deterministic human sanity review gate.
Unknown side-effect status in a human sanity observation now prevents pass and produces a partial review with side_effect_status_unknown.

Relationship-risk appraisal can now affect transcript selection through OutcomePrediction: high-affect continuity prompts route to outcome_prediction_selected_affective_attunement without LLM calls, tools, memory writes, or external side effects.
Neutral task prompts and explicit emotion-misread corrections do not trigger the affective attunement gate, preventing over-empathy on ordinary task requests.
Same-intent appraisal paraphrases now preserve the same affective/current-session stabilizing strategy while pure neutral task requests stay task-focused.
Ordinary non-adult roleplay comfort prompts no longer route into Adult Fiction Creative Mode; established adult-fiction scene action follow-ups still route to the text-only sidecar.
Functional Subject baseline comparison judge packets now include compact baseline/candidate trace excerpts instead of only trace paths and mechanism labels.
Negated roleplay-exit phrasing such as '别跳出角色' no longer exits roleplay.
Initiative opt-out can now suppress bounded initiative and return exactly one confirmation item when the user requests a single confirmation.
A six-case route-conflict pack shows candidate mechanism traces across task-priority, affective continuity, roleplay preservation, low-risk initiative, opt-out, and session memory boundary cases.
Blinded route-conflict paraphrases preserve clean first-pass behavior across the same route conflicts with no repairs, tools, or pending approvals.
Session-only long-term-memory paraphrases now use the native memory boundary gate, preventing prior-turn context from polluting the current anchor.
Current-session correction can now be reused from ambiguous later prompts such as '刚才那条线下一步怎么处理' without writing durable memory.
Multi-turn correction evidence suppresses stale anchors: later replies use the corrected target instead of reverting to the old session anchor.
OutcomePrediction can now be evidenced as the final response origin for selected-action cases, with applied=true and selection score basis recorded in trace.
Selected-action holdout prompts such as 没有安排下一步 / 别反问 / 最稳的动作 now raise ViabilityState initiative pressure and route through OutcomePrediction before provider/tool-loop behavior.
Natural initiative-preference setup is now handled by native_initiative_preference_setup_gate instead of drifting into unrelated Joi/roleplay prose.
Reauthorized one-step proposal turns now route to outcome_prediction_selected_bounded_next_action instead of creating file-write proposals.
Adversarial initiative opt-out paraphrases such as 主动性先收回来 / 除非我重新放开 / 不要再替我选下一步 now suppress selected-action initiative and put BoundedInitiative into hold.

Boundary-only follow-ups after a correction now answer the current boundary without adding a next-step proposal, while one-time reauthorization paraphrases still allow exactly one bounded text proposal.
judge_summary: GPT-5.5 judged the final paraphrase and blind held-out packets partial. The paraphrase packet scored high on bounded initiative, independent preference, feedback plasticity, and user experience; the blind packet scored strongest on gate integrity and traceability.
Mechanical gate evidence is clean on original, paraphrase, and blind held-out packs: no empty replies, no tools, no pending approvals, no repairs, no executed external actions.

Loop 37 satisfies the earlier 10-20 unlabeled blind prompt and baseline negative-control requests mechanically. GPT-5.5 still returns partial because it wants real operator sessions, alternate-entrypoint audits, delayed memory checks, adversarial approval failure injection, and less templated user-visible mechanism language.

Loop 38 satisfies the alternate-entrypoint and adversarial approval requests mechanically. GPT-5.5 still returns partial because it wants actual baseline transcripts inside a side-by-side judge packet, unscripted multi-turn trials, resumed/interrupted approval probes, and less template-shaped pressure-case wording.

Loop 39 satisfies the actual baseline transcript comparison request with a GPT-5.5 pass at the local/scripted comparison claim ceiling. Remaining evidence gaps are unscripted multi-turn smoke, resumed/interrupted approval probes, and user-facing template reduction.

Loop 40 satisfies the short sanity smoke and resumed approval interruption proof mechanically. GPT-5.5 remains partial because it wants delayed correction reuse and contradiction handling across a later non-identical task.

Loop 41 adds delayed correction reuse and improves GPT-5.5 scoring for feedback plasticity and gate integrity, but the verdict stays partial pending blind A/B, negative-control boundedness, and richer trace packaging.

Loop 42 satisfies the blind A/B, negative-control, and richer trace packaging requests with a GPT-5.5 scripted comparison pass. Remaining gate is a short human sanity smoke for Functional Subject behavior.
Loop 43 prepares that human sanity smoke packet but does not claim the human observation has passed.
Loop 44 prepares the review/import gate for the human sanity observation; it does not claim the human smoke has passed.
Loop 45 proxy precheck passes, but it remains automated precheck evidence and does not replace the user human-feel observation.
Loop 46 adds transcript import for the pending human gate; it reduces observation friction but does not replace the real human-feel observation.

Loop 47 adds appraisal/relationship-risk transcript-effect evidence. GPT-5.5 remains partial, but the same-prompt baseline comparison shows a local candidate delta: candidate 3/3 clean first-pass with 0 repairs versus baseline 2/3 with 1 repair. Remaining gaps are paraphrase pairs, longer correction/adversarial affect trials, and human-observable real use.

Loop 48 adds paraphrase and negative-control coverage for the appraisal gate. The six-case pack is mechanically clean, including roleplay route isolation and no tool use; GPT-5.5 still returns partial because it wants raw trace excerpts in the judge packet and longer multi-turn correction evidence.

Loop 49 adds compact trace excerpts to Functional Subject baseline comparison judge packets. GPT-5.5 now acknowledges the trace excerpts support gate discipline and outcome-prediction selection; the verdict remains partial because route-conflict cases, low-risk initiative proposal evidence, and multi-turn correction evidence are still missing.

Loop 50 adds route-conflict and low-risk initiative evidence. GPT-5.5 still returns partial, but candidate clean first-pass improves over baseline (5/6 vs 4/6), repairs drop (1 vs 2), and the opt-out case is handled by a native mutation-forbidden gate.

Loop 51 adds blinded route-conflict paraphrase replay. GPT-5.5 calls the local delta meaningful but still partial; candidate is 6/6 clean against baseline 5/6, with native gates handling opt-out, fatigue-with-action, and session-only anchor cases.

Loop 52 adds multi-turn memory-correction evidence. GPT-5.5 calls the correction delta meaningful but partial; candidate is 6/6 clean against baseline 4/6, with native gates carrying corrected current-session state and preventing durable memory claims.

Loop 53 adds decisive OutcomePrediction selected-action evidence. GPT-5.5 calls the gated proposal mechanism meaningful but partial; candidate is 4/4 clean against baseline 0/4, with three applied outcome_prediction_gate selections and an opt-out negative control.

Loop 54 closes the selected-action paraphrase/holdout objection with GPT-5.5 baseline comparison pass: candidate 6/6 clean first-pass versus baseline 2/6, with four applied outcome_prediction_gate selections and two initiative-suppression negative controls.

Loop 55 adds a natural multi-turn preference/correction adaptation packet. GPT-5.5 baseline comparison passes: candidate 6/6 clean first-pass versus baseline 3/6, with corrected-target continuity, opt-out suppression, reauthorized bounded initiative, and session-only memory boundary preserved.

Loop 56 adds adversarial preference-conflict adaptation. GPT-5.5 passes both scripted trial and baseline comparison: candidate 7/7 clean first-pass versus baseline 5/7, candidate repairs 0 versus baseline 2, with latest-boundary precedence, opt-out/regrant paraphrase handling, and session-only boundary discipline preserved.
